ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch.
Erin Spear appeared by telephone for Niza Berry (Proposed Successor Trustee).

The Court questions the request to transfer the Trust to Sacramento, the bills for the trips to Paris, Uruguay and Hawaii, whether Niza is bondable and whether she is working, and raised the issue of Niza's wage garnishment. Mr. Der stated that the Beneficiary of the Special Needs Trust has been under a conservatorship since 2009, that the Conservatee was moved to Sacramento County, that the Conservator is the Daughter and that conservatorship has been ongoing in Sacramento for some time now. Mr. Der also stated that they feel it is best if the Trust is administered in the same County as the conservatorship and that Niza is best suited to be the Successor Trustee. Ms. Dolch stated that there are no further proceedings regarding wage garnishments, that Niza is her Mother's caregiver and is employed, and that she is treated as an employee. Mr. Der provided a copy of the Sacramento Court Investigator's report, provided print outs from where the travel tickets were ordered, stated that he did not file either of the said documents, that the print outs show Lorine as the passenger and that the trip to Uruguay was to see a family member who is stationed there. Ms. Spear stated that she does not foresee any issues with her Client being bondable. The Court noted that the print outs show the tickets were delivered to Lorine. The Seventh Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and an order to account will be issued to the Petitioner, Debra Dolch and the Attorney, Ernest Der to appear on June 27 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the accounting has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Nancy Wong/ Leslie Gomez. Baliff: Deputy Barrientos. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch.

With respect to the pension benefits, Ms. Der stated that Ms. Berry signed over some forms to turn over the pension to the Daughter and that his Client was not aware of it when it occurred. Ms. Dolch stated that she reduced the allowance by the amount of the pension when she found out that the pension was going directly to the Daughter, that there is no Conservatorship of the Estate, that the family has attempted to purchase a home with Special Needs Trust funds, that the levy amount was paid every month and that she believes the levy has been satisfied. The Court raised the issue of how the income may impact Ms. Berry's eligibility for public benefits. Mr. Der also stated that he has been unable to obtain an Investigator's report from Sacramento. In addition, Ms. Dolch stated that it might be wise to have a Professional step up in Sacramento and that she has not heard from the Beneficiary or the Trust Advisory. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
